figures.caption: Fig. 5. Illustration of the fetching behavior: (a) a naive approach that leads to early rendezvous causes a counter-productive reaction by green; (b) with MPPI, rendezvous is avoided until the optimal time.

Fig. 8. Simulation snapshots of a 4-robot case in a 5-obstacle environment. Colored rings denote sensing range; circles encode behavior as: yellow = complete task; robot’s color = fetching that robot; none = explore.
Fig. 6. Outcome distribution across the 6 simulated scenarios. Our approach is deemed successful if the time to completion is the same (yellow) or improved (green) compared to the baseline.
Fig. 7. A comparison of the task completion time for our approach versus the baseline when a decision to fetch was made by a robot within the MRS (improved trials from Fig. 6).
Fig. 3. Example of the frontier selection process for exploration.
Fig. 9. Experiment results for a 3 robots case.
Fig. 4. Illustration of the tree search-based behavior selection method.
